docs.title: Welcome to jimmy's documentation!
docs.text: jimmy is a tool to import your notes from different formats to Markdown.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• ✅ Several supported input formats
  • ✅ Markdown + Frontmatter output
    • Compatible with any text editor
    • Can be imported to Joplin/Obsidian/...
    • Preserves resources, tags and note links when possible
  • ✅ Offline
  • ✅ Open Source
  • ✅ Cross-platform
  • ✅ Standalone (no Docker, Python or NodeJS installation required)
  • ❎ No AI

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
Download jimmy here: Linux | Windows | MacOS

If the executable doesn't work, you might try one of the options below.

OS Error Message Alternative Comment Linux version 'GLIBC_2.38' not found Download supports systems with glibc >=2.31 MacOS zsh: bad CPU type in executable Download supports the x86_64 architecture

docs.title: Supported Apps
docs.text:
Export data from your app and convert it to Markdown. For details, click on the links.

A Anki Anytype B Bear C Cacher CherryTree Clipto ColorNote D Day One Dynalist E Evernote F Facebook FuseBase, Nimbus Note G Google Docs Google Keep J Joplin jrnl N Notion O Obsidian Q QOwnNotes R RedNotebook S Simplenote Standard Notes Synology Note Station T Textbundle, Textpack Tiddlywiki Tomboy-ng, Gnote Turtl W Wordpress Z Zettelkasten Zim Zoho Notebook

docs.title: Supported Formats
docs.text:
Import a single file or a folder (recursively). Files of these formats will be converted to Markdown. The formats can be mixed. For example you can import a folder with two Asciidoc files and one docx file. The conversion result will be a folder with three Markdown files and the corresponding attachments.

A Asciidoc C CSV D DocBook docx E eml EPUB F Fountain H HTML J Jupyter Notebook M Markdown MediaWiki O ODT OPML R reStructuredText RTF T txt2tags

docs.title: General Usage
docs.text:
flowchart LR
    A[App 1] -->|Backup| M
    B[App 2] -->|Export| M
    C[...] --> M
    D[Filesystem] --> M
    M(ZIP archive/JSON/Folder) --> N
    N{jimmy} --> O(Markdown + Frontmatter)
    O -->|Import| P[Joplin]
    O -->|Import| Q[Obsidian]
    O --> R[...]
    O --> S[Editor, e. g. VSCode]
  1. Export/backup notes from your note application
  2. Run jimmy, which converts your notes to Markdown
  3. Import the result to Joplin/Obsidian or even use any editor like VSCode

For detailed instructions, see the page of the specific format.

docs.title: Quickstart
docs.text:
1. Import from text files or import from specific apps, like Google Keep
  2. Verify that everything was converted properly. The Markdown notes should be available in a new folder named like YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS - Jimmy Import.

docs.title: Import Instructions
docs.text: This page describes how you can import the notes that you just converted to Markdown.

docs.title: Any Editor
docs.text: Open the root folder in the editor.

docs.title: Joplin
docs.text:
Markdown files can be imported to Joplin as described on the website:

  1. In Joplin, go to File -> Import and choose:
    • MD - Markdown (Directory) if you converted without frontmatter. This is the default.
    • MD - Markdown + Front Matter (Directory) if you converted with frontmatter. You can add frontmatter by using the --frontmatter joplin argument.
  2. Select the root folder.

docs.title: Logseq
docs.text:
Since Logseq doesn't support folders, this will only work with "flat exports". I. e. where all Markdown files are in the root folder. It works for example with Google Keep.

  1. Use the arguments --output-folder "pages" --global-resource-folder "../assets". This exports the Markdown notes to the pages folder and the resources next to it in a assets folder. Just like it is in Logseq. A complete command could look like jimmy-cli-linux takeout-20240401T160516Z-001.zip --format google_keep --output-folder "pages" --global-resource-folder "../assets"
  2. Either copy the folders to your Logseq graph or open them as new graph to check how it looks.

docs.title: Obsidian
docs.text:
Markdown files can be imported to Obsidian as described on the website:

  • Copy the root folder to you vault or open it as a new vault.
  • Add metadata in the frontmatter by using the --frontmatter obsidian argument.

docs.title: QOwnNotes
docs.text:
1. Use the arguments --local-resource-folder attachments --local-image-folder media. This is not required, but aligns with the internal structure of QOwnNotes.
  2. Copy the root folder to your note folder or open it as a new note folder.
  3. If you have subfolders, enable Note -> Settings -> Use note subfolders.
  4. If you want to convert tags:
    1. Use the argument --frontmatter qownnotes
    2. Detect the front matter tags by installing the epsilon-notes-tags or yaml-nested-tags plugin.

A complete command could look like jimmy-cli-linux takeout.zip --format google_keep --frontmatter qownnotes --local-resource-folder attachments --local-image-folder media.

docs.title: Notion
docs.text: Markdown files can be imported to Notion as described on the website. This will only import Markdown notes, though. Importing resources seems to be not supported.

docs.title: UpNote
docs.text: Import from Markdown will most likely work as described on the website. The feature is behind a paywall, so I couldn't test it. Be aware that export to Markdown is behind a paywall, too!

docs.title: VNote
docs.text: Choose "Import Folder" as described in the documentation.

docs.title: Filters
docs.text: There are some simple filters to select which data to import. For more complex filtering, a separate plugin or script should be used.

docs.title: Available Filters
docs.text:
All filters can be used with wildcard. The filters are case-sensitive.

  • Exclude or include specific notes (--exclude-notes, --include-notes)
  • Exclude or include notes with specific tags (--exclude-notes-with-tags, --include-notes-with-tags)
  • Exclude or include the tags themself (--exclude-tags, --include-tags)

To check the exact command, visit the help page (--help).

docs.title: Limitations
docs.text: Filtering by notebooks is not possible, because nesting can yield some ambiguities.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# show the help
jimmy-cli-linux --help

# import specific nots only
jimmy-cli-linux obsidian_vault/ --format obsidian --include-notes "Sample note" "Second sample note"

# exclude notes with a tag
jimmy-cli-linux obsidian_vault/ --format obsidian --exclude-notes-with-tags "ignore this tag"

# don't import any tags
jimmy-cli-linux obsidian_vault/ --format obsidian --exclude-tags "*"

docs.title: Silent Mode
docs.text: Can be activated by combining the two CLI arguments --stdout-log-level CRITICAL --no-progress-bars.

docs.title: Customizing the Output
docs.text: The output can be customized in the following ways.

docs.title: Frontmatter
docs.text: Include Markdown frontmatter by --frontmatter. Currently, Joplin and Obsidian compatible frontmatter (--frontmatter joplin respectively --frontmatter obsidian) can be generated. Alternatively, all available metadata can be included (--frontmatter all).

docs.title: Output Folder
docs.text: The output folder can be specified by --output-folder OUTPUT_FOLDER.

docs.title: Resource Location
docs.text:
Resources are stored by default next to the Markdown file. When using --local-resource-folder FOLDER, the resources are stored relative to the Markdown files. For example in an media folder, like in QOwnNotes.

When using --global-resource-folder FOLDER, the resources are stored relative to the output folder. This means the resources of all files are stored in the same folder. This folder can be also outside of the root folder. Like ../assets in Logseq.

docs.title: Show the Note Tree
docs.text: The note tree in the intermediate format can be shown by --print-tree.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
1. Simply show the imported note tree:
$ jimmy-cli-linux .cache/arbitrary_folder/arbitrary_folder/ --print-tree
[09/17/24 17:39:20] INFO     Importing notes from ".cache/arbitrary_folder/arbitrary_folder"
                    INFO     Start parsing
[09/17/24 17:39:21] INFO     Finished parsing: 3 notebooks, 7 notes, 3 resources, 1 note links
Note Tree
└── 📘 20240917T153920Z - Jimmy Import
    └── 📘 arbitrary_folder
        ├── 📖 sample
        │   ├── 🎴 test.png
        │   └── 🔗 link to second_sample_file
        ├── 📖 plaintext
        ├── 📖 test
        ├── 📖 Big-Fish
        └── 📘 nested_arbitrary_folder
            ├── 📖 LibreOffice Writer
            │   └── 🎴 1000000100000B5F00000449B7E060775BACC2F0.png
            ├── 📖 asciidoc
            │   └── 🎴 test\_image
            └── 📖 second_sample_file
                    INFO     Start filtering
                    INFO     Finished filtering: 3 notebooks, 7 notes, 3 resources, 1 note links
                    INFO     Start writing to file system
                    INFO     Converted notes successfully to Markdown. Please verify that everything was converted correctly.
                    INFO     Feel free to open an issue on Github, write a message at the Joplin forum or an email.

Notebooks  100%|████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████| 3/3 [00:00<00:00]
Notes      100%|████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████| 7/7 [00:00<00:00]
Resources  100%|████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████| 3/3 [00:00<00:00]
Note Links 100%|████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████| 1/1 [00:00<00:00]
  1. Verify that the tags are really excluded:
$ jimmy-cli-linux .cache/obsidian/obsidian_vault --format obsidian --exclude-tags "*" --print-tree
[09/17/24 17:42:14] INFO     Importing notes from ".cache/obsidian/obsidian_vault"

Note Tree Filtered

docs.title: Contact
docs.text:
• General discussion at the
    • Joplin forum
    • Obsidian Forum
  • Report a bug or request a new feature at github

docs.title: As non-programmer
docs.text:
• You can help the project by providing an export file. This helps to identify and fix issues.
  • Add documentation or fix spelling mistakes
  • Test the app
  • Write in forums of your note application if Jimmy works for you

docs.title: How to add a new format?
docs.text:
Here is a good example commit.

The brief workflow is:

  1. Discuss the format at the forum or github
  2. Implement at src/
  3. Provide an example file and add a test
  4. Lint with lint.sh
  5. Document at docs/

docs.title: Why Markdown?
docs.text: To provide a flexible base for migrating your notes to the app of your choice.

docs.title: Why enlighten and not tqdm for progress bars?
docs.text: enlighten did integrate easier with pythons logging.

docs.title: Sort all iterators with arbitrary order
docs.text:
Reproducibility is more important than memory usage and speed.

# good
for item in sorted(file_or_folder.iterdir()):

# bad
for item in file_or_folder.iterdir():

docs.title: Why pyinstaller and not nuitka?
docs.text: I did have a bit experience in setting up pyinstaller. The size of the final executable seems to be much smaller with pyinstaller, too.

docs.title: Why is the executable so large?
docs.text:
Pandoc is included and is standalone ~144 MB large. This has the biggest impact on the size. The module sizes in particular can be analyzed by using the following code snippet in the pyinstaller spec file:

coll = COLLECT(
    exe,
    a.binaries,
    a.datas,
    strip=False,
    upx=True,
    upx_exclude=[],
    name='jimmy_cli',
)

The resulting files can be listed and ordered by size by:

$ du -lh --max-depth=2 dist/jimmy_cli | sort -h
12K     dist/jimmy_cli/_internal/src
24K     dist/jimmy_cli/_internal/wheel-0.44.0.dist-info
40K     dist/jimmy_cli/_internal/Markdown-3.7.dist-info
44K     dist/jimmy_cli/_internal/anyblock_exporter
60K     dist/jimmy_cli/_internal/cryptography-43.0.3.dist-info
60K     dist/jimmy_cli/_internal/setuptools
108K    dist/jimmy_cli/_internal/ossl-modules
164K    dist/jimmy_cli/_internal/puremagic
296K    dist/jimmy_cli/_internal/charset_normalizer
2,4M    dist/jimmy_cli/_internal/yaml
11M     dist/jimmy_cli/_internal/cryptography
15M     dist/jimmy_cli/_internal/lib-dynload
144M    dist/jimmy_cli/_internal/pypandoc
213M    dist/jimmy_cli/_internal
262M    dist/jimmy_cli

docs.title: Why cryptography and not pycryptodome?
docs.text: They worked both at the first implementation. cryptography made a slightly better impression, so it was chosen.

docs.title: Format Conversion Paths
docs.text:
graph TD;
    File -- Plain Formats (plaintext,markdown) --> Markdown;

    File -- Pandoc Unsupported Formats (anytype,colornote,tid,zim,zkn3 bbcode) --> pandoc_unsupported[Pyparsing / External Lib];
    pandoc_unsupported --> Markdown;

    %% pandoc paths
    File -- HTML --> Beautifulsoup;
    Beautifulsoup -- Preprocessed HTML String --> Pandoc;
    File -- Pandoc Supported Formats --> Pandoc;
    File -- Encapsulated Pandoc Supported Formats (eml,enex,notion,zoho) --> note_extraction[Extract Note];
    note_extraction -- Pandoc Supported Formats --> Pandoc;
    Pandoc --> Markdown;

docs.title: Intermediate Format
docs.text:
• HTML:
  • Easily modifyable by beautifulsoup and others.
  • Supports wide range of elements that can be "reduced" to Markdown.
  • No additional dependency (beautifulsoup is used already).
  • Pandoc AST:
  • Python: Panflute and pandocfilters aren't up-to-date (problems with tables especially).
  • Lua: Learning curve, second scripting language in this repo.
  • General: Some filters need some preprocessing (in HTML), like iframes.

docs.title: Get sample exports
docs.text: Documentation how to get sample exports from "difficult" apps. Difficult means that I don't have access through my phone, Linux desktop or Windows VM.

docs.title: All Apple only Apps
docs.text: TODO

docs.title: FuseBase/Nimbus Note
docs.text:
Server backups are available only with Plus or Ultimate plans.

Server backups contain the whole workspace. At least single page exports are possible.

docs.title: Samsung Notes
docs.text:
Doesn't work yet

  1. Install Genymotion
  2. Disable all running adb servers if necessary (https://support.genymotion.com/hc/en-us/articles/360002734657-The-Virtual-device-is-stuck-on-booting-status)
  3. Install and startphone (for example Samsung Galaxy S10)
  4. Install "Open Gapps" (button on the right)
  5. Install a browser
  6. https://m.apkpure.com/samsung-notes/com.samsung.android.app.notes

docs.title: Synology Note Station
docs.text:
1. Go to the demo page and press the "Test" button
  2. Open Note Station by Apps -> Note Station on the top left
  3. Files can be transferred through the file system

docs.title: Turtl
docs.text:
• Linux app crashes always
  • Android app still works

docs.title: Wikimedia
docs.text: Export at https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Spezial:Exportieren.

docs.title: More Note Apps

Loose collection of note apps/messengers/wikis/formats that could be implemented. For concrete implementations and discussions, a github issue should be used.

Remnote - doc - script - example file Reor Rightnote Roam format doc Samsung Notes script Samsung only Scrivener - doc - thread Signal https://github.com/marph91/jimmy/issues/8 Siyuan doc Skiff Pages Slite doc (Markdown, paid only) Stickies https://github.com/marph91/jimmy/issues/4 Supernotes doc Tana thread Takenote Export via settings to zipped md and json No tags/metadata? docs.title: Anki
docs.text: This page describes how to convert Anki cards to Markdown.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: .apkg

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export as described at the website
    1. Choose "Deck (.apkg)"
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Convert to Markdown. Example: jimmy-cli-linux MEILLEUR_DECK_ANGLAIS_3000.apkg --format anki
  4. Import to your app

docs.title: Import Structure
docs.text:
• Decks are converted to folders.
  • Cards are converted to Markdown files. The content is the "answer" data.
  • Referenced resources (audio, images and other files) are converted.

A converted page looks like:

docs.title: Known Limitations
docs.text:
• Nested decks are not tested and most likely not working.
  • HTML formatting is lost. It's too complext to cover all HTML templates properly.

docs.title: Anytype
docs.text: This page describes how to convert notes from Anytype to Markdown.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: .zip or folder

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export as described at the website
    1. Choose "Any-Block"
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Convert to Markdown. Example: jimmy-cli-linux Anytype.20241112.175339.64 --format anytype
  4. Import to your app

docs.title: Acknowledgements
docs.text: The converter is based on https://github.com/jfcostello/AnyBlock-To-Markdown. Thanks for developing it!

docs.title: Known Limitations
docs.text: Images and attachments aren't converted.

docs.title: Bear
docs.text: This page describes how to convert notes from Bear to Markdown.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: .bear2bk

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export as described at the website
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Convert to Markdown. Example: jimmy-cli-linux backup.bear2bk --format bear
  4. Import to your app

docs.title: Cacher
docs.text: This page describes how to convert notes from Cacher to Markdown.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: .json

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export as described at the website
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Convert to Markdown. Example: jimmy-cli-linux cacher-export-202406182304.json --format cacher
  4. Import to your app

docs.title: Import Structure
docs.text:
• Cacher labels are converted to tags.
  • Cacher snippets are converted to folders.
  • Cacher files are converted to Markdown notes.

docs.title: Known Limitations
docs.text:
• Only Markdown files are converted for now.
  • Files attached to snippets are not exported.

docs.title: CherryTree
docs.text: This page describes how to convert notes from CherryTree to Markdown.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: .ctd

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export as described at the website
    1. Choose "Export" -> "Export to CherryTreeDocument" -> "All the Tree" -> "XML, Not Protected (.cdt)"
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Convert to Markdown. Example: jimmy-cli-linux cherry.ctd --format cherrytree
  4. Import to your app

docs.title: Import Structure
docs.text:
• CherryTree nodes are converted to Markdown notes.
  • If a CherryTree node contains sub nodes, a folder is created as container. It has the same name as the corresponding note.

docs.title: Known Limitations
docs.text:
• Cherrytree checked (☑) and crossed checkboxes (☒) are converted to Markdown checked checkboxes ([x]).
  • Cherrytree latex is converted to a Markdown code block.
  • Some cherrytree elements are not inserted at their original location, but at the end of the note. This is a limitation of cherrytree's export.
  • Cherrytree anchors are not converted.

docs.title: Clipto
docs.text: This page describes how to convert notes from Clipto to Markdown.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: .json

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export your notes from the mobile app 1
    1. Settings -> Time Machine -> Backup to file
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Convert to Markdown. Example: jimmy-cli-linux clipto_backup_240401_105154.json --format clipto
  4. Import to your app

  1. Export works with the mobile app only. See https://github.com/clipto-pro/Desktop/issues/21#issuecomment-537401330. ↩

docs.title: ColorNote
docs.text: This page describes how to convert notes from ColorNote to Markdown.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: .backup

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export as described at the website
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Convert to Markdown. Example: jimmy-cli-linux colornote-20241013.backup --format colornote --password 0000
    1. The default password for automatic backups is 0000.
  4. Import to your app

docs.title: Day One
docs.text: This page describes how to convert notes from Day One to Markdown.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: .zip

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export as described at the website
    1. Choose "Day One JSON (.zip)"
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Convert to Markdown. Example: jimmy-cli-linux Export-Tagebuch.zip --format day_one
  4. Import to your app

docs.title: Import Structure
docs.text:
• Each day is converted to a notebook.
  • Entries are converted to notes and grouped into the corresponding notebook of that day.
  • Referenced Audios, PDF, photos and videos are imported as resources.

docs.title: Known Limitations
docs.text:
• Unreferenced photos are not converted.
  • Photos that are references by multiple notes are only imported once (i. e. in one note). This seems to be a bug in the Day One export.

docs.title: Default Import
docs.text: This page describes how to convert notes from single files or folders (recursively) to Markdown.

docs.title: Supported Formats
docs.text:
The default import covers the following formats:

  • Every format that is supported by pandoc. Some formats may need some tweaking, though. A few examples:
    • Comma-separated values (CSV)
    • DocBook
    • DOCX
    • electronic publication (EPUB)
    • Hypertext Markup Language (HTML)
    • Jupyter Notebook
    • MediaWiki
    • OpenDocument-Text (ODT)
    • Outline Processor Markup Language (OPML)
    • reStructuredText (RST)
    • Rich Text Format (RTF))
    • txt2tags
  • asciidoc (requires asciidoctor installed and in path)
  • Email (eml)
  • Fountain:
    • There is a built-in Joplin plugin that can be activated in the settings.
    • There is a Obsidian plugin.
  • Markdown (Commonmark)
  • txt

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Install jimmy
  2. Convert to Markdown. Examples: 
    # import a single file supported by pandoc
jimmy-cli-linux libre_office_document.odt

# import multiple files
jimmy-cli-linux plaintext.txt markdown.md

# import all files in a folder recursively
jimmy-cli-linux path/to/folder
  3. Import to your app

docs.title: Import Structure
docs.text:
• Folders stay folders
  • Files are converted to Markdown notes

docs.title: Dynalist
docs.text: This page describes how to convert notes from Dynalist to Markdown.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: .zip

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export as described at the website
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Convert to Markdown. Example: jimmy-cli-linux dynalist-backup-2024-04-12.zip --format dynalist
  4. Import to your app

docs.title: Evernote
docs.text: This page describes how to convert notes from Evernote to Markdown.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: .enex or folder of .enex files

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export as described at the website
    1. Choose "enex"
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Convert to Markdown. Example: jimmy-cli-linux backup.enex --format evernote
    1. To decrypt encrypted notes, specify your password, like jimmy-cli-linux backup.enex --format evernote --password 1234
  4. Import to your app

docs.title: Import Structure
docs.text:
• Each .enex file is converted to a folder.
  • Notes inside the .enex file are converted to Markdown files.
  • Note links are recovered by matching the note name. This might not work sometimes, if the name was changed after creating the link.

docs.title: Facebook
docs.text: This page describes how to convert posts and messages from Facebook to Markdown.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: .zip

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export as described at at the website
    1. Choose JSON
    2. The export may take some time. For a 450 MB file it took two days.
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Convert to Markdown. Example: jimmy-cli-linux facebook-xyz-07.07.2024-m9lv24pS.zip --format facebook
  4. Import to your app

docs.title: Import Structure
docs.text:
• Posts and messages are stored in separate notebooks.
  • Each post is converted to a separate note, starting with the creation date (YYYY-MM-DD).
  • Each conversation is a note. Messages are concatenated inside the note and separated by the day. Conversations may be splitted to prevent too big notes.
  • Referenced resources (audio, gif, photos, videos and other files) are converted.

docs.title: Known Limitations
docs.text:
The import was tested with many messages (450 MB, >50000 messages) and only a few posts (~20 posts). The import may be not robust enough yet.

  • Shared posts are not converted.
  • Profile images, stories and shorts are not converted.
  • Group chats are not converted, since Facebook creates a new file everytime a person joins or leaves. It's not possible to merge them by ID.

docs.title: Google Docs
docs.text: This page describes how to convert notes from Google Docs to Markdown.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extensions: .zip, .tgz

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export via Google Takeout
    1. Select "Drive"
    2. Select the folders you want to export
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Convert to Markdown. Example: jimmy-cli-linux takeout-20240401T160556Z-001.zip --format google_docs
  4. Import to your app

docs.title: Google Keep
docs.text: This page describes how to convert notes from Google Keep to Markdown.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extensions: .zip, .tgz

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export via Takeout as described at the website
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Convert to Markdown. Examples:
    1. jimmy-cli-linux takeout-20240401T160516Z-001.zip --format google_keep
    2. jimmy-cli-linux takeout-20240401T160556Z-001.tgz --format google_keep
  4. Import to your app

docs.title: Import Structure
docs.text:
• Each note is converted to a Markdown file.
  • Links, attachments and tags are preserved.
  • Annotations are appended at the end of a note.

docs.title: Known Limitations
docs.text: • The indentation level of task lists is not exported from Google Keep.

docs.title: Joplin
docs.text: This page describes how to convert notes in Joplin's JEX format to Markdown.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: .jex

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export as described at the website
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Convert to Markdown. Example: jimmy-cli-linux 29_04_2024.jex --format joplin
  4. Import to your app

docs.title: jrnl
docs.text: This page describes how to convert notes from jrnl to Markdown.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: .json

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export as described at the website
    1. Export as json to preserve metadata.
    2. Example: jrnl --format json --file myjournal.json
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Convert to Markdown. Example: jimmy-cli-linux myjournal.json --format jrnl
  4. Import to your app

docs.title: Import Structure
docs.text: Each journal entry is converted to a separate note in a flat notebook.

docs.title: FuseBase
docs.text: This page describes how to convert notes from FuseBase (formerly Nimbus Note) to Markdown.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: Folder with .zip files

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export as described at the website
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Convert to Markdown. Example: jimmy-cli-linux nimbus_note/Example/ --format nimbus_note
  4. Import to your app

docs.title: Known Limitations
docs.text:
• Tags aren't included in single page exports.
  • Note links aren't converted.

If you have a complete workspace backup (premium feature), feel free to share. It may lead to tag and note link support.

docs.title: Notion
docs.text: This page describes how to convert notes from Notion to Markdown.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: .zip

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export as described at the website
    1. Choose "Markdown and CSV" and check "Create folder for sub-pages" when exporting.
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Convert to Markdown. Example: jimmy-cli-linux 72a2f31c-3a46-4b44-826d-ae046e693551_Export-d609fb9f-43a4-475d-ba88-1db3e9e6bcd2.zip --format notion
  4. Import to your app

docs.title: Import Structure
docs.text: Subpages are linked and converted to Markdown files in a subfolder.

docs.title: Obsidian
docs.text: This page describes how to convert notes from Obsidian to Markdown.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: Folder with .md files

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Install jimmy
  2. Convert to Markdown. Example: jimmy-cli-linux obsidian_vault/ --format obsidian
  3. Import to your app

docs.title: QOwnNotes
docs.text: This page describes how to convert notes from QOwnNotes to Markdown.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: Folder with .md files (notes), a media subfolder (containing resources) and a notes.sqlite file (containing tags)

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Install jimmy
  2. Convert to Markdown. Example: jimmy-cli-linux qownnotes_folder/ --format qownnotes
  3. Import to your app

docs.title: Import Structure
docs.text: • Markdown style links ([Link to Markdown Cheatsheet](Markdown Cheatsheet.md)) and QOwnNotes style links (<Markdown Cheatsheet.md>) as described here are converted.

docs.title: Known Limitations
docs.text: • Encrypted notes aren't converted.

docs.title: RedNotebook
docs.text: This page describes how to convert notes from RedNotebook to Markdown.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: .zip or data folder with text files

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Create a backup of your notes as described at the website or specify the data folder (for example $HOME/.rednotebook/data)
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Convert to Markdown. Examples:
    1. jimmy-cli-linux RedNotebook-Backup-2024-09-15.zip --format rednotebook
    2. jimmy-cli-linux ~/.rednotebook/data/ --format rednotebook
  4. Import to your app

docs.title: Known Limitations
docs.text: Images and files are not part of the backup. They will only be included, if the files exist in your filesystem.

docs.title: Simplenote
docs.text: This page describes how to convert notes from Simplenote to Markdown.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: .zip

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export as described at the website
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Convert to Markdown. Example: jimmy-cli-linux notes.zip --format simplenote
  4. Import to your app

docs.title: Standard Notes
docs.text: This page describes how to convert notes from Standard Notes to Markdown.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: .zip

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export as described at the website
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Convert to Markdown. Example: jimmy-cli-linux "Standard Notes Backup - Sun Apr 28 2024 12_56_55 GMT+0200.zip" --format standard_notes
  4. Import to your app

docs.title: Import Structure
docs.text: Notes in "Super" format are converted to Markdown. Other notes are taken as-is.

docs.title: Known Limitations
docs.text: Note links, attachments and folders are not implemented yet, since they require a subscription.

docs.title: Synology Note Station
docs.text: This page describes how to convert notes from Synology Note Station to Markdown.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: .nsx

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export as described at the website
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Convert to Markdown. Example: jimmy-cli-linux notestation-test-books.nsx --format synology_note_station
  4. Import to your app

docs.title: Known Limitations
docs.text: Inline charts are not converted.

docs.title: Textbundle
docs.text: This page describes how to convert notes from Textbundle to Markdown.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extensions: .textbundle, .textpack or folder of .textbundle and .textpack

docs.title: Apps that support Textbundle export
docs.text:
Selection from the textbundle website:

  • Bear
  • Craft
  • Ulysses
  • Wordpress
  • Zettlr

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export from any of the mentioned apps
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Convert to Markdown. Examples:
    1. jimmy-cli-linux "Textbundle Example v1.textbundle/" --format textbundle
    2. jimmy-cli-linux "example.textpack" --format textbundle
    3. jimmy-cli-linux folder/with/textbundles/ --format textbundle
  4. Import to your app

docs.title: Tiddlywiki
docs.text: This page describes how to convert notes from TiddlyWiki to Markdown.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: .json, .tid of folder of .tid files

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export as described at the website
    1. Choose "JSON file" if you want to export the complete wiki
    2. Choose "TID text file" if you want to export a single tiddler only. Resources and internal links won't be converted in this case.
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Convert to Markdown. Examples:
    1. jimmy-cli-linux tiddlers.json --format tiddlywiki
    2. jimmy-cli-linux tiddlers.tid --format tiddlywiki
    3. jimmy-cli-linux /folder/to/tiddlers --format tiddlywiki
  4. Import to your app

docs.title: Known Limitations
docs.text: Note content is in TiddlyWiki's WikiText format. It is converted, but Markdown supports only a subset. For example Javascript functions won't work in the converted Markdown anymore.

docs.title: Todo.txt
docs.text: This page describes how to convert notes from Todo.txt to Markdown.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: .txt

docs.title: Apps that support Todo.txt
docs.text:
• The main todo.txt app
  • Other projects

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export from any of the mentioned apps
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Convert to Markdown. Example: jimmy-cli-linux examples_from_readme.txt --format todo_txt
  4. Import to your app

docs.title: Todoist
docs.text: This page describes how to convert notes from Todoist to Markdown.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: .csv

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export as described at the website
    1. Uncheck "Use relative data" when exporting.
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Convert to Markdown. Example: jimmy-cli-linux Privates.csv --format todoist
  4. Import to your app

docs.title: Known Limitations
docs.text:
• Finished todo's are not exported at all.
  • Subtasks are converted to regular notes. I. e. they lose their indentation.
  • Markdown is not rendered in note titles.

docs.title: Gnote
docs.text: This page describes how to convert notes from Tomboy-ng and Gnote to Markdown.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Tomboy-ng Website
  • Gnote Website
  • Typical extension: .note file or folder with .note files

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Install jimmy
  2. Convert to Markdown. Examples:
    1. jimmy-cli-linux ~/.local/share/tomboy-ng/ --format tomboy_ng
    2. jimmy-cli-linux ~/.local/share/gnote --format tomboy_ng
    3. jimmy-cli-linux tomboy-ng/5E74990A-E93E-4A11-AEA2-0814A37FEE1A.note --format tomboy_ng
  3. Import to your app

docs.title: Known Limitations
docs.text:
• Note links are not exported.
  • Multiple formats are not converted properly.

docs.title: Toodledo
docs.text: This page describes how to convert notes from Toodledo to Markdown.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: .csv

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export as described at the website
    1. Uncheck "Use relative data" when exporting.
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Convert to Markdown. Examples:
    1. jimmy-cli-linux toodledo_completed_240427.csv --format toodledo
    2. jimmy-cli-linux toodledo_current_240427.csv --format toodledo
    3. jimmy-cli-linux toodledo_notebook_240427.csv --format toodledo
  4. Import to your app

docs.title: Known Limitations
docs.text: subtasks and files are not implemented, since they require a subscription.

docs.title: Turtl
docs.text: This page describes how to convert notes from Turtl to Markdown.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: .json

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export as shown at the website
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Convert to Markdown. Example: jimmy-cli-linux turtl-backup.json --format turtl
  4. Import to your app

docs.title: Import Structure
docs.text:
• Spaces are converted to folders.
  • Boards are converted to subfolders of the corresponding space.
  • Notes are converted to Markdown files in the corresponding board.
  • Tags and files are converted.

docs.title: Wordpress
docs.text: This page describes how to convert notes from Wordpress to Markdown.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: .xml

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export as shown at the website
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Convert to Markdown. Example: jimmy-cli-linux mywordpresswebsite.WordPress.2024-12-17.xml --format wordpress
  4. Import to your app

docs.title: Xit
docs.text: This page describes how to convert notes from [x]it! to Markdown.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: .xit

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Install jimmy
  2. Convert to Markdown. Example: jimmy-cli-linux example.xit --format xit
  3. Import to your app

docs.title: Import Structure
docs.text:
• xit groups are converted to folders
  • xit items are converted to Markdown todos
  • Priority, tags and due dates are converted to metadata and removed from the todo title

docs.title: Zettelkasten
docs.text: This page describes how to convert notes from Zettelkasten (zkn3) to Markdown.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: .zkn3

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Install jimmy
  2. Convert to Markdown. Example: jimmy-cli-linux test_zettelkasten.zkn3 --format zettelkasten
  3. Import to your app

docs.title: Import Structure
docs.text:
Zettelkasten supports Markdown export. However, it doesn't convert everything and the note links and attachments get lost. Most data can be preserved, when using the original .zkn3 location in the filesystem without any export.

  • Each zettel is converted to a separate note.
  • The note body of Zettelkasten is converted from BBCode to Markdown.
  • Resources and attachments are converted, if the original folders are next to the .zkn3 file. I. e. img for images and attachments for attachments.
  • Note sequences (Folgezettel) are linked at the end of the note.

docs.title: Known Limitations
docs.text: Some formatting is not converted, because it can't be expressed in pure Markdown (e. g. alignment).

docs.title: Zim
docs.text: This page describes how to convert notes from Zim Wiki to Markdown.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: Folder with .txt files

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Install jimmy
  2. Convert to Markdown. Example: jimmy-cli-linux zim/folder --format zim
  3. Import to your app

docs.title: Import Structure
docs.text:
Zim does a good job in exporting to Markdown. If the built-in export is fine for you, you don't need to use Jimmy.

Jimmy doesn't use pandoc for conversion and applies some additional tweaks:

  • Consistently use ATX style headings (starting with #).
  • Consistently use spaces instea of tabs.
  • Page title and creation date are removed from the note body. They are instead stored in the metadata respectively the filename. The metadata can be included by a front matter.
  • Convert Zim checklists to Markdown checklists (- [ ]) instead of Markdown lists with signs (- ☐). The checklist states are converted as described below:
    • Done and not done are converted to - [x].
    • All other states are converted to -[ ].

docs.title: Zoho Notebook
docs.text: This page describes how to convert notes from Zoho Notebook to Markdown.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
• Website
  • Typical extension: .zip

docs.title: Instructions
docs.text:
1. Export as described at the website
    1. Export as HTML
  2. Install jimmy
  3. Convert to Markdown. Example: jimmy-cli-linux Notebook_14Apr2024_1300_html.zip --format zoho_notebook
  4. Import to your app
